title: Newsflash #4 is out — added to NST on Monday, it dropped 18% on Friday
author: StackMotive

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
An 18% drop right after being added to NST isn’t that unusual, especially if there was a run‑up into the inclusion. A lot of names get bid up on anticipation, then see a “sell the news” reaction once the actual addition happens.

I’d be looking at a few things before drawing conclusions:

- Was volume elevated on Friday? If so, that could indicate institutional repositioning rather than pure panic selling.  
- Any broader sector weakness or macro headlines that might have amplified the move?  
- Valuation going into the news — was it already stretched?

Index additions can create short-term volatility, but they don’t change fundamentals. If the underlying thesis hasn’t changed, this could just be noise. If the drop coincided with new guidance or material info, that’s different.

Curious if anyone here was tracking options flow or short interest around the inclusion. That might give more context to the move.

comments.author: StackMotive
comments.body:
Good points and you’re right, the sell-the-news pattern on index additions is real. But in this case there was more going on.
The platform I’m running had been firing sustained bearish CONVERGENCE alerts on NST every single day from 6 March, a full seven days before the crash. Price had dropped below SMA50, MACD histogram was deepening negative daily (-0.41 by 9 March, -0.51 by 10 March), and the signals were HIGH severity throughout. By the evening of 12 March it had fired 10+ bearish CONVERGENCE alerts.
On your volume question, yes Friday volume came in at 2.1x normal. RSI hit 29.7 at close. The platform flagged both.
On short interest, I don’t have clean short volume ratio data for NST in the pre-crash window so I won’t claim I do. What I did have was seven days of technical deterioration while the company was publicly reporting record EBITDA. The technicals and the fundamentals were telling completely different stories.
The honest takeaway: the DCA rule fired on price action alone on Monday 9 March. The broader signal stack had been bearish for three days at that point. That’s the tension that will be at the core of Newsflash #5, Newsflash #4 documents the rule-based add, #5 will cover what the full signal picture actually looked like and what I should have done with it !!
